name: Sounds Only
description: No words allowed — make the sound and let your team guess! Noises, animals, machines, and more.

cards.charadesHint: Make a deep rolling rumbling sound that grows louder and then crashes suddenly
Make the classic two-tone alternating wail of an emergency vehicle speeding past
Make the escalating wail of a very unhappy infant — start quiet, build to full volume
Make the long slow groan of a heavy old door opening very slowly in the dark
Make the rising hiss and rumble that builds until it clicks off
Make the sharp repetitive bark of an excited medium-sized dog
Make the low continuous rumbling vibration of a very contented cat on your lap
Make the roaring throttle of a motorcycle engine building up to full speed
Make the sharp explosive crash of a glass hitting a tiled floor and shattering
Make the deep rhythmic rasping rumble of someone in deep heavy sleep
Make the rapid rattling buzz of a zip being quickly pulled open or shut
Make the precise steady tick-tock of an old mechanical clock on a mantelpiece
Make the final swirling glug of water disappearing down a plughole
Make the rhythmic four-beat clop of hooves at full gallop — getting faster and faster
Make the rhythmic swoosh and slosh followed by the spinning roar of a spin cycle
Make the explosive POP followed by fizzing bubbling overflow
Make the magnificent blaring honk of an elephant raising its trunk
Make the rapid clatter and ding of keys striking paper ending with a carriage return
Make the roar of a huge crowd reacting to something brilliant — building from silence to eruption
Make the continuous vibrating hum of a hive of bees all at once
Make the soft pattering that builds to a drumming of rain hitting glass
Make the unmistakable sound — any variety is acceptable, creativity encouraged
Make the rolling rumble of a ball followed by the explosive crash of all ten pins
Make the full chaotic sequence — screech, static, squeal, handshake — of connecting to the internet in 1999
Make the mournful rising howl of a wolf under a full moon — long, haunting, powerful
Make the piercing high-pitched screech of a mic held too close to a speaker
Make the sharp hiss and release of a carbonated can being cracked open
Make the rising drone of a hoover sucking up everything in its path
Make the deep long mournful blast of a ship's foghorn disappearing into mist
Make the soft sustained sibilant hiss of a snake defending itself
Make the satisfying ripping tear of a strip of Velcro being pulled apart
Make the steady lub-dub beat of a resting heart — then speed it up for running
Make the alternating loop of a car alarm cycling through its various obnoxious sounds
Make the sudden jarring scratch and stop of a vinyl record being yanked
Make the soft repetitive low cooing of a pigeon doing its thing on a window ledge
Make the warm irregular snap and pop of a real wood fire burning
Make the high-pitched whirring drill that fills everyone with immediate dread
Make the rush, screech, hiss of brakes, and the doors opening of a train pulling in
Make the satisfying rapid-fire popping of an entire sheet of bubble wrap
Make the resonant slow clang of large bells ringing from a tower
Make the ethereal echoing hoot of an owl in the quiet dark of night
Make the rhythmic thwop-thwop-thwop of rotor blades chopping through the air
Make the sporadic then accelerating rapid-fire pops of a microwave bag of popcorn
Make the rushing swirling whoosh of water evacuating a toilet bowl
Make the rhythmic rush and pull-back of waves on a shoreline — repeat slowly
Make the harsh flatulent BZZZT of a game show wrong answer sound
Make the rapid clashing ring of metal on metal — back and forth, parry and thrust
Make the low gurgling growl of a very hungry stomach demanding to be fed
Make the explosive sharp crack of a gun fired to start a race
Make the sharp supersonic snap of a whip cutting through the air
Make the wheeze and push-pull of a French accordion playing a melancholy tune
Make the high-pitched groan of a floorboard under someone trying very hard to be quiet
Make the on-the-hour cuckoo-cuckoo call of the little bird emerging from its clock
Make the rapid stuttering burst of automatic fire
Make the long deep mournful oscillating calls of whales communicating across vast ocean depths
Make the rhythmic soft crunch of feet pressing into packed snow
Make the distorted roaring sustain of an electric guitar wailing through an amplifier
Make the merciless relentless beeping that drags you from the best sleep of your life
Make the explosive bang of a door swung hard enough to shake the whole house
Make the combination of wind in leaves, distant bird calls, and an eerie unsettling quiet
